### Clone and Install Convex Tutorial Project

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/tutorial

Clones the example Convex tutorial repository from GitHub and installs its Node.js dependencies. This is the initial setup step required to begin the tutorial.

```bash
git clone https://github.com/get-convex/convex-tutorial.git

cd convex-tutorial

npm install
```

--------------------------------

### Create TanStack Start app with Convex template

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/quickstart/tanstack-start

Quick setup command to scaffold a new TanStack Start project with Convex pre-configured.

```bash
npm create convex@latest -- -t tanstack-start
```

--------------------------------

### Clone and run RAG example project

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/agents/rag

Setup and execute the RAG example to demonstrate adding content, searching, generating text, and introspecting context with various filter options.

```Bash
git clone https://github.com/get-convex/rag.git

cd rag

npm run setup

npm run example
```

--------------------------------

### Get URL and Metadata for Stored File (Initial Example)

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/api/interfaces/server.StorageReader

This example demonstrates how to get a URL for a stored file and retrieve its metadata using the system table, which is the preferred method.

```javascript
// Get a URL for a stored file:

const url = await ctx.storage.getUrl(storageId);

if (url) {

  // Use the URL (e.g., return it to the client)

}



// Get file metadata via the system table (preferred over deprecated getMetadata):

const metadata = await ctx.db.system.get("_storage", storageId);

// metadata: { _id, _creationTime, sha256, size, contentType? }
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize New Project with Convex Auth

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/auth/convex-auth

Use this command to start a new project from scratch, selecting 'React (Vite)' and 'Convex Auth' during the setup process.

```bash
npm create convex@latest
```

--------------------------------

### Start Development Server

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/auth/authkit

Navigate to your project directory and run this command to start the development server, which initiates the Convex+WorkOS onboarding.

```bash
cd my-app  # or whatever you name the directory

npm run dev
```

--------------------------------

### Install AuthKit TanStack Start SDK

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/auth/authkit/add-to-app

npm command to install the WorkOS AuthKit SDK for TanStack Start applications.

```Bash
npm install @workos/authkit-tanstack-react-start

```

--------------------------------

### Setup Convex Agent Mode with bun

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/ai

Initialize dependencies and start Convex dev server in Agent Mode using bun package manager. The CONVEX_AGENT_MODE=anonymous flag limits permissions while allowing codegen and testing.

```bash
bun i

CONVEX_AGENT_MODE=anonymous bun x convex dev --once
```

--------------------------------

### Run Convex Agent Example

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/agents/files

This snippet provides the commands to clone, set up, and run the Convex agent example project locally.

```bash
git clone https://github.com/get-convex/agent.git

cd agent

npm run setup

npm run dev
```

--------------------------------

### Create TanStack Start app

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/quickstart/tanstack-start

Initialize a new TanStack Start application using the official create-start-app command.

```bash
npx create-start-app@latest
```

--------------------------------

### Install Convex TanStack Query Adapter

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/client/tanstack/tanstack-query

Install the `@convex-dev/react-query` library using npm.

```bash
npm i @convex-dev/react-query
```

--------------------------------

### Install Convex Client and Server Libraries

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/quickstart/svelte

Navigates into the newly created project directory and installs the `convex` and `convex-svelte` packages required for integration.

```bash
cd my-app && npm install convex convex-svelte
```

--------------------------------

### Install Convex and Nuxt module

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/quickstart/nuxt

Installs the Convex client library and the `convex-nuxt` module into your project.

```bash
cd my-nuxt-app && npm install convex && npx nuxi module add convex-nuxt
```

--------------------------------

### Install Convex Client and Server Libraries

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/quickstart/android

Initialize a new npm project and install the convex package, which includes both client and server libraries.

```bash
npm init -y

npm install convex
```

--------------------------------

### Install Convex and React Query dependencies

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/quickstart/tanstack-start

Install the Convex client library along with React Query and TanStack Router SSR integration packages required for TanStack Start.

```bash
npm install convex @convex-dev/react-query @tanstack/react-router-ssr-query @tanstack/react-query
```

--------------------------------

### Basic HTTP Router Setup

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/functions/http-actions

Create an HTTP router and define a simple GET endpoint that returns a response. Export the router as default from convex/http.ts.

```TypeScript
import { httpRouter } from "convex/server";

import { httpAction } from "./_generated/server";



const http = httpRouter();



http.route({

  path: "/",

  method: "GET",

  handler: httpAction(async (ctx, request) => {

    return new Response(`Hello from ${request.url}`);

  }),

});

export default http;
```

--------------------------------

### Generate Example App Code and Deploy with Convex CLI

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/components/authoring

Command to generate code for the example application and deploy it, with an optional flag to type-check components.

```bash
npx convex dev --typecheck-components # optionally type-check the components
```

--------------------------------

### Start a Local Convex Development Deployment

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/cli/local-deployments

Use this command to start a local Convex deployment for an existing project. The --once flag ensures it runs a single time.

```bash
npx convex dev --local --once
```

--------------------------------

### Setup Convex Agent Mode with npm

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/ai

Initialize dependencies and start Convex dev server in Agent Mode for remote coding agents. The CONVEX_AGENT_MODE=anonymous flag limits permissions while allowing codegen and testing.

```bash
npm i

CONVEX_AGENT_MODE=anonymous npx convex dev --once
```

--------------------------------

### Navigate to app folder

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/chef

Change directory into your downloaded and renamed app folder to begin setup.

```bash
cd ~/<app folder>
```

--------------------------------

### Install Convex client and dotenv

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/quickstart/nodejs

Installs the `convex` package for interacting with Convex and `dotenv` for loading environment variables.

```bash
npm install convex dotenv
```

--------------------------------

### Start React Native App

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/quickstart/react-native

Run `npm start` to launch the Expo development server and view your React Native application on a device or emulator.

```bash
npm start
```

--------------------------------

### Install npm dependencies

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/chef

Install all project dependencies required for the Convex app to run.

```bash
npm i
```

--------------------------------

### Install dependencies for Convex Chef project

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/chef/migration-guide

Navigate to your app folder and install all required Node.js dependencies using npm.

```bash
cd ~/<app folder>

npm i
```

--------------------------------

### Example push_config Audit Log

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/production/integrations/log-streams/legacy-event-schema

This example shows the structure of an audit log entry for a 'push_config' action, detailing added and removed modules.

```json
{

  "_topic": "_audit_log",

  "_timestamp": 1695066350531,

  "action": "push_config",

  "actionMetadata": {

    "modules": {

      "added": ["ffmpeg.js", "fetch.js", "test.js"],

      "removed": ["removed.js"]

    }

  }

}
```

--------------------------------

### Install Convex client and server libraries

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/quickstart/rust

Installs the Convex npm package for backend development and the Convex Rust client library, Tokio runtime, and dotenvy for environment variable management.

```bash
npm init -y && npm install convex && cargo add convex tokio dotenvy
```

--------------------------------

### Conductor deployment creation setup

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/production/multiple-deployments

Conductor.json setup command that creates a dev deployment with team, project, user, and workspace name in the reference. Replace my-team and my-project with your actual slugs.

```json
{
  "scripts": {
    "setup": "npm ci && npx convex deployment create --type=dev my-team:my-project:dev/$USER-conductor/$CONDUCTOR_WORKSPACE_NAME --select"
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Install Convex library in Remix

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/quickstart/remix

Navigate into your newly created Remix project directory and install the official Convex npm package.

```bash
cd my-remix-app && npm install convex
```

--------------------------------

### Cursor local worktrees deployment creation setup

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/production/multiple-deployments

Worktrees.json setup command for Cursor that creates a dev deployment with team, project, user, and directory name in the reference. Replace my-team and my-project with your actual slugs.

```json
{
  "setup-worktree": [
    "npm ci",
    "npx convex deployment create --type=dev my-team:my-project:dev/$USER-cursor/$(basename \"$PWD\") --select"
  ]
}
```

--------------------------------

### Codex app deployment creation setup

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/production/multiple-deployments

Setup script for Codex app environments that creates a dev deployment with team, project, and user-specific reference. Replace my-team and my-project with your actual slugs.

```bash
npm ci && npx convex deployment create --type=dev my-team:my-project:dev/$USER-codex/$(basename "$(dirname "$CODEX_WORKTREE_PATH")") --select
```

--------------------------------

### Install Convex Client and Server Libraries

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/quickstart/python

Installs the Convex npm package for backend development and the Convex Python client library along with python-dotenv for environment variables.

```bash
cd my-app && npm init -y && npm install convex && venv/bin/pip install convex python-dotenv
```

--------------------------------

### Install AuthKit and Convex WorkOS Integration

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/auth/authkit/add-to-app

Install the required npm packages for AuthKit React SDK and Convex WorkOS integration.

```bash
npm install @workos-inc/authkit-react @convex-dev/workos
```

### Install Convex client and server library

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/quickstart/bun

Add the `convex` package to your Bun project dependencies.

```bash
bun add convex
```

### Install Clerk Next.js SDK

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/auth/clerk

Install the Clerk Next.js SDK to integrate Clerk's authentication functionalities into your Next.js application.

```bash
npm install @clerk/nextjs
```

--------------------------------

### Example File Metadata Document

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/file-storage/file-metadata

An example of the JSON document structure returned when querying file metadata from the `_storage` system table.

```json
{
  "_creationTime": 1700697415295.742,
  "_id": "3k7ty84apk2zy00ay4st1n5p9kh7tf8",
  "contentType": "image/jpeg",
  "sha256": "cb58f529b2ed5a1b8b6681d91126265e919ac61fff6a367b8341c0f46b06a5bd",
  "size": 125338
}
```

--------------------------------

### Example OpenAPI Specification (YAML)

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/client/open-api

An example of the `convex-spec.yaml` file structure generated by the `convex-helpers` CLI, detailing the API endpoints and schema.

```yaml
# convex-spec.yaml

openapi: 3.0.3

info:

  title: Convex App - OpenAPI 3.0

  version: 0.0.0

  servers:

    - url: "{hostUrl}"

  description: Convex App API

  ...
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize a new npm project

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/quickstart/script-tag

Use `mkdir` and `npm init` to set up a new project directory.

```bash
mkdir my-project && cd my-project && npm init -y
```

--------------------------------

### GET /projects/:project_id/list_preview_deploy_keys

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/management-api/list-preview-deploy-keys

Lists all preview deploy keys for the specified project.

```APIDOC
## GET /projects/:project_id/list_preview_deploy_keys

### Description
Lists all preview deploy keys for the specified project.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
/projects/:project_id/list_preview_deploy_keys

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **project_id** (string) - Required - The ID of the project.

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
```

--------------------------------

### Install AuthKit Next.js SDK

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/auth/authkit/add-to-app

This command installs the necessary npm packages for integrating WorkOS AuthKit with a Next.js application, including `@workos-inc/authkit-nextjs` and `@convex-dev/workos`.

```bash
npm install @workos-inc/authkit-nextjs @convex-dev/workos
```

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/auth/clerk

Install the necessary Clerk React SDK package into your project to use Clerk's authentication components and hooks.

```bash
npm install @clerk/react
```

### Set up Convex development deployment

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/quickstart/bun

Run this command to log in, create a Convex project, and sync your backend functions with the dev deployment.

```bash
bunx convex dev
```

--------------------------------

### current_storage_usage Event Example

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/production/integrations/log-streams

Example JSON structure for a storage usage event showing document, index, vector, text, file, and backup storage metrics. Use this format to parse incoming storage events from Convex deployments.

```json
{
  "topic": "current_storage_usage",
  "timestamp": 1715973841548,
  "total_document_size_bytes": 104857600,
  "total_index_size_bytes": 10485760,
  "total_vector_storage_bytes": 5242880,
  "total_text_storage_bytes": 1048576,
  "total_file_storage_bytes": 52428800,
  "total_backup_storage_bytes": 209715200,
  "total_system_table_document_size_bytes": {
    "_storage": 1048576,
    "_scheduled_functions": 2097152
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Use an NPM Package in a Convex Query

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/functions/query-functions

This example shows how to import and use an installed NPM package, like `faker.js`, within a Convex query function to generate data.

```typescript
import { query } from "./_generated/server";
import { faker } from "@faker-js/faker";

export const randomName = query({
  args: {},
  handler: () => {
    faker.seed();
    return faker.person.fullName();
  }
});
```

--------------------------------

### GET /getMessagesByAuthor - Retrieve Messages by Author

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/api/classes/server.HttpRouter

Example HTTP action that retrieves messages filtered by author. This endpoint demonstrates importing HTTP action handlers from separate files.

```APIDOC
## GET /getMessagesByAuthor

### Description
Retrieves messages filtered by a specific author. This endpoint demonstrates the pattern of importing HTTP action handlers from separate module files.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
/getMessagesByAuthor

### Implementation Pattern
```javascript
// In convex/http.js
import { getMessagesByAuthor } from "./getMessagesByAuthor";

http.route({
  path: "/getMessagesByAuthor",
  method: "GET",
  handler: getMessagesByAuthor,
});

// Handler is defined in convex/getMessagesByAuthor.js
```

### Notes
- Handler is imported from a separate file (getMessagesByAuthor.js)
- Demonstrates modular organization of HTTP action handlers
- Actual query parameters and response format depend on the imported handler implementation
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize Git repository

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/chef

Set up Git version control for your project; the downloaded code includes a .gitignore file.

```bash
git init
```

```bash
git add --all
```

```bash
git commit -m "Initial commit"
```

--------------------------------

### `useSmoothText` with Conditional Streaming

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/agents/streaming

This example shows how to conditionally start streaming text immediately based on a message's status. It's useful for mixed streaming and non-streaming messages.

```typescript
import { useSmoothText, type UIMessage } from "@convex-dev/agent/react";



function Message({ message }: { message: UIMessage }) {

  const [visibleText] = useSmoothText(message.text, {

    startStreaming: message.status === "streaming",

  });

  return <div>{visibleText}</div>;

}
```

--------------------------------

### Define an internal query to get a user

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/api/modules/server

This example demonstrates how to define an internal query using `internalQuery` to fetch user data by `userId`. Internal queries are only callable from other Convex functions, typically via `ctx.runQuery`.

```javascript
import { internalQuery } from "./_generated/server";
import { v } from "convex/values";

// Only callable from other Convex functions:
export const getUser = internalQuery({
  args: { userId: v.id("users") },
  returns: v.union(
    v.object({
      _id: v.id("users"),
      _creationTime: v.number(),
      name: v.string(),
      email: v.string(),
    }),
    v.null(),
  ),
  handler: async (ctx, args) => {
    return await ctx.db.get("users", args.userId);
  },
});
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize Convex Data Import

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/database/import-export/import

Starts the Convex data import process. Use `--help` to view all available options.

```shell
npx convex import
```

--------------------------------

### Convex: Using Math.random() and Date.now() in Mutations

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/functions/runtimes

This example demonstrates the deterministic behavior of `Math.random()` and `Date.now()` within Convex mutations. `globalRand` and `globalNow` are frozen at deploy time, while `Date.now()` is frozen at function execution start and `Math.random()` generates new values per call.

```javascript
const globalRand = Math.random(); // `globalRand` does not change between runs.

const globalNow = Date.now(); // `globalNow` is the time when Convex functions were deployed.



export const updateSomething = mutation({

  args: {},

  handler: () => {

    const now1 = Date.now(); // `now1` is the time when the function execution started.

    const rand1 = Math.random(); // `rand1` has a new value for each function run.

    // implementation

    const now2 = Date.now(); // `now2` === `now1`

    const rand2 = Math.random(); // `rand1` !== `rand2`

  },

});
```

--------------------------------

### Create a new Bun project directory

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/quickstart/bun

Use this command to initialize a new Bun project in a dedicated directory.

```bash
mkdir my-project && cd my-project && bun init -y
```

--------------------------------

### Install Convex Agent Skills with npx CLI

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/ai/agent-skills

Use npx skills to selectively install individual Convex skills or install all available skills at once. Skills are installed into .agents/skills/ and automatically picked up by compatible agents.

```bash
# Choose which skills to install

npx skills add get-convex/agent-skills



# Or install all of them at once

npx skills add get-convex/agent-skills --all
```

--------------------------------

### GET ctx.storage.getUrl

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/api/interfaces/server.StorageActionWriter

Get the URL for a file in storage by its `Id<"_storage">`. The GET response includes a standard HTTP Digest header with a sha256 checksum.

```APIDOC
## GET ctx.storage.getUrl

### Description
Get the URL for a file in storage by its `Id<"_storage">`. The GET response includes a standard HTTP Digest header with a sha256 checksum.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
ctx.storage.getUrl

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **storageId** (GenericId<"_storage">) - Required - The `Id<"_storage">` of the file to fetch from Convex storage.

### Request Example
```javascript
const url = await ctx.storage.getUrl(storageId);
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- A URL which fetches the file via an HTTP GET, or `null` if the file no longer exists.

#### Response Example
```json
"https://example.com/file_url"
```
```

--------------------------------

### Create a new Remix project

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/quickstart/remix

Use the Remix CLI to initialize a new Remix application with a specified project name.

```bash
npx create-remix@latest my-remix-app
```

--------------------------------

### GET /deployments/:deployment_name/custom_domains

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/management-api/list-custom-domains

Get all custom domains configured for a deployment.

```APIDOC
## GET /deployments/:deployment_name/custom_domains

### Description
Get all custom domains configured for a deployment.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
/deployments/:deployment_name/custom_domains

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **deployment_name** (string) - Required - The name of the deployment.

### Response
#### Success Response (200)

```

### Create sample data file

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/quickstart/tanstack-start

Create a JSONL file with sample task data to import into the Convex database. Each line is a separate JSON object.

```jsonl
{"text": "Buy groceries", "isCompleted": true}
{"text": "Go for a swim", "isCompleted": true}
{"text": "Integrate Convex", "isCompleted": false}
```

{"text": "Buy groceries", "isCompleted": true}

{"text": "Go for a swim", "isCompleted": true}

{"text": "Integrate Convex", "isCompleted": false}
```

```bash
npm create vue@latest my-vue-app
```

```bash
cd my-vue-app && npm install convex convex-vue
```

```bash
npm i @convex-dev/agent

Install the convex package to enable Convex client and server functionality in your React app.

```bash
cd my-app && npm install convex
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize ConvexClient and subscribe to updates

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/api/classes/browser.ConvexClient

Initializes a new ConvexClient instance with a Convex deployment URL and demonstrates how to subscribe to real-time updates for a query using the `onUpdate` method.

```javascript
const client = new ConvexClient("https://happy-otter-123.convex.cloud");

const unsubscribe = client.onUpdate(api.messages.list, {}, (messages) => {
  console.log(messages[0].body);
});
```

--------------------------------

### Install Convex npm package

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/understanding/workflow

Installs the Convex client library as a dependency in your project, enabling interaction with Convex services.

```bash
npm i convex
```

--------------------------------

### Seed Dev Deployments with Sample Data

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/database/import-export/import

Examples for populating a development deployment using either a full backup ZIP file or by importing a single table from a JSONL/CSV file.

```shell
# full backup - exported from prod or another dev deployment.

npx convex import seed_data.zip
```

```shell
# Import single table from jsonl/csv

npx convex import --table <table name> data.jsonl
```

Install the Convex CLI package via npm to access all command-line tools for project management.

```bash
npm install convex
```

```bash
npm run dev

```bash
npm install --save-dev convex-test vitest @edge-runtime/vm
```

npm install @faker-js/faker

Initializes a new SvelteKit project named 'my-app' using the latest Svelte CLI, prompting for configuration options.

```bash
npx sv@latest create my-app
```

### Create a new React app with Vite

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/quickstart/react

Use `npm create vite` to initialize a new React project with TypeScript.

```bash
npm create vite@latest my-app -- --template react-ts
```

--------------------------------

### Set up Convex Client in Svelte Layout

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/quickstart/svelte

Initializes the Convex client in `src/routes/+layout.svelte` using `setupConvex` and the public Convex URL, making it available throughout the Svelte app.

```svelte
<script lang="ts">

	import { PUBLIC_CONVEX_URL } from '$env/static/public';

	import { setupConvex } from 'convex-svelte';



	const { children } = $props();

	setupConvex(PUBLIC_CONVEX_URL);

</script>



{@render children()}
```

--------------------------------

### Create Convex deployment with options

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/production/multiple-deployments

Creates a dev deployment with a descriptive reference, US region, 7-day expiration, and --select flag to use it for subsequent npx convex commands.

```bash
npx convex deployment create dev/james/feature-payment-integration --type=dev --region us --expiration "in 7 days" --select
```

--------------------------------

### Create a new Cargo project

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/quickstart/rust

Initializes a new Rust project named 'my_app' and navigates into its directory.

```bash
cargo new my_app

cd my_app
```

### Install Convex Helpers npm package

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/client/open-api

Install the `convex-helpers` package, which provides a CLI command for generating an OpenAPI specification from your Convex deployment.

```bash
npm install convex-helpers
```

--------------------------------

### GET /query

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/public-deployment-api/public-query-get

Execute a query function via GET request. This endpoint allows clients to invoke query functions and retrieve the results.

```APIDOC
## GET /query

### Description
Execute a query function via GET request.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
/query

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
Query executed successfully.
```

--------------------------------

### Deploy and run a preview function

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/cli

Deploy Convex functions and execute a specific function after deployment using the --preview-run option.

```bash
npx convex deploy --preview-run myFunction
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize a new Node.js project

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/quickstart/nodejs

This command creates a new directory, initializes an npm project, and sets the package type to 'module'.

```bash
mkdir my-project && cd my-project && npm init -y && npm pkg set type="module"
```

--------------------------------

### Example NumberDocument for Union Type

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/database/schemas

An example document conforming to the 'NumberDocument' type defined in a union schema, with a 'kind' literal and a number 'value'.

```json
{
  "kind": "NumberDocument",
  "value": 123
}
```

--------------------------------

### Convex Clients with Bun

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/client/javascript/bun

This example demonstrates how to use both ConvexHttpClient for point-in-time queries and ConvexClient for subscriptions and mutations within a Bun environment. It shows fetching messages, subscribing to updates, and sending a new message.

```typescript
import { ConvexHttpClient, ConvexClient } from "convex/browser";

import { api } from "./convex/_generated/api.js";



// HTTP client

const httpClient = new ConvexHttpClient(process.env.CONVEX_URL);

httpClient.query(api.messages.list).then((messages) => {

  console.log(messages);

});



// Subscription client

const client = new ConvexClient(process.env.CONVEX_URL);

const unsubscribe = client.onUpdate(api.messages.list, {}, (messages) =>

  console.log(messages),

);

await Bun.sleep(1000);

client.mutate(api.messages.send, {}, { body: "hello!", author: "me" });

await Bun.sleep(1000);
```

--------------------------------

### Example StringDocument for Union Type

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/database/schemas

An example document conforming to the 'StringDocument' type defined in a union schema, with a 'kind' literal and a string 'value'.

```json
{
  "kind": "StringDocument",
  "value": "abc"
}
```

--------------------------------

### Convex Client Initialization Parameters

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/api/classes/browser.ConvexHttpClient

Details the parameters required and optional for configuring a Convex client instance.

```APIDOC
## Convex Client Initialization Parameters

### Description
This section describes the parameters used to initialize a Convex client. These parameters configure the client's connection to the Convex deployment, logging behavior, and authentication.

### Parameters
- **address** (string) - Required - The url of your Convex deployment, often provided by an environment variable. E.g. `https://small-mouse-123.convex.cloud`.
- **options?** (Object) - Optional - An object of options.
    - **skipConvexDeploymentUrlCheck** (boolean) - Optional - Skip validating that the Convex deployment URL looks like `https://happy-animal-123.convex.cloud` or localhost. This can be useful if running a self-hosted Convex backend that uses a different URL.
    - **logger** (object | boolean) - Optional - A logger or a boolean. If not provided, logs to the console. You can construct your own logger to customize logging to log elsewhere or not log at all, or use `false` as a shorthand for a no-op logger. A logger is an object with 4 methods: log(), warn(), error(), and logVerbose(). These methods can receive multiple arguments of any types, like console.log().
    - **auth** (string) - Optional - A JWT containing identity claims accessible in Convex functions. This identity may expire so it may be necessary to call `setAuth()` later, but for short-lived clients it's convenient to specify this value here.
    - **fetch** (function) - Optional - A custom fetch implementation to use for all HTTP requests made by this client.
```

--------------------------------

### take

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/api/interfaces/server.OrderedQuery

Execute the query and return the first `n` results.

```APIDOC
## take

### Description
Execute the query and return the first `n` results.

### Method
SDK Method

### Endpoint
N/A

### Parameters
- **n** (number) - Required - The number of items to take.

### Request Body
N/A

### Request Example
N/A

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **Result** (Promise<DocumentByInfo<TableInfo>[]>) - An array of the first `n` results of the query (or less if the query doesn't have `n` results).

#### Response Example
N/A
```

--------------------------------

### Convex Imports and Setup

Source: https://www.convex.dev/llms.txt

This snippet shows the necessary imports for defining Convex functions and integrating with OpenAI. It sets up the Convex API and the OpenAI client.

```typescript
import {
  query,
  mutation,
  internalQuery,
  internalMutation,
  internalAction,
} from "./_generated/server";
import { v } from "convex/values";
import OpenAI from "openai";
import { internal } from "./_generated/api";
```

--------------------------------

### Install ESLint Plugins for .eslintrc.js

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/eslint

Install the TypeScript ESLint plugin and Convex ESLint plugin as development dependencies for projects using the deprecated `.eslintrc.js` format.

```npm
npm i @typescript-eslint/eslint-plugin @convex-dev/eslint-plugin --save-dev
```

--------------------------------

### Install Convex ESLint Plugin for ESLint 9

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/eslint

Install the Convex ESLint plugin as a development dependency for projects using ESLint 9 (flat config).

```npm
npm i @convex-dev/eslint-plugin --save-dev
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Convex Components in convex.config.ts

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/components/using

Define and install Convex Components in your application's configuration file. You can install multiple instances of the same component with different names.

```typescript
import { defineApp } from "convex/server";
import agent from "@convex-dev/agent/convex.config.js";

const app = defineApp();

app.use(agent);
app.use(agent, { name: "agent2" });
//... Add other components here

export default app;
```

--------------------------------

### Create and configure ConvexReactClient

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/client/react

Initialize a ConvexReactClient instance with your Convex backend deployment URL. This client maintains the connection to your backend.

```typescript
import { ConvexProvider, ConvexReactClient } from "convex/react";



const convex = new ConvexReactClient("https://<your domain here>.convex.cloud");
```

--------------------------------

### StorageReader.getUrl

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/api/interfaces/server.StorageReader

Get the URL for a file in storage by its `Id<"_storage">`. The GET response includes a standard HTTP Digest header with a sha256 checksum.

```APIDOC
## StorageReader.getUrl

### Description
Get the URL for a file in storage by its `Id<"_storage">`. The GET response includes a standard HTTP Digest header with a sha256 checksum.

### Parameters
#### Request Body
- **storageId** (GenericId<"_storage">) - Required - The `Id<"_storage">` of the file to fetch from Convex storage.

### Request Example
```javascript
const url = await ctx.storage.getUrl(storageId);
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **url** (string | null) - A URL which fetches the file via an HTTP GET, or `null` if the file no longer exists.

#### Response Example
```json
"https://example.com/file_url_123"
```
```

--------------------------------

### Create ConvexReactClient

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/api/modules/react

Initialize a Convex client with your deployment URL, typically loaded from an environment variable.

```JavaScript
import { ConvexReactClient } from "convex/react";



// typically loaded from an environment variable

const address = "https://small-mouse-123.convex.cloud"

const convex = new ConvexReactClient(address);
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize ConvexClient with deployment URL

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/client/swift

Create a single ConvexClient instance for your application's lifetime, passing your Convex backend deployment URL. The connection is established lazily on first method call.

```Swift
import ConvexMobile



let convex = ConvexClient(deploymentUrl: "https://<your domain here>.convex.cloud")
```

--------------------------------

### GET ctx.storage.getUrl (Deprecated)

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/api/interfaces/server.StorageActionWriter

Deprecated: Get the URL for a file in storage by its `StorageId`. Passing a string is deprecated, use `storage.getUrl(Id<"_storage">)` instead.

```APIDOC
## GET ctx.storage.getUrl (Deprecated)

### Description
Deprecated: Get the URL for a file in storage by its `StorageId`. Passing a string is deprecated, use `storage.getUrl(Id<"_storage">)` instead.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
ctx.storage.getUrl

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **storageId** (T extends string) - Required - The `StorageId` of the file to fetch from Convex storage.

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- A url which fetches the file via an HTTP GET, or `null` if it no longer exists.

#### Response Example
```json
"https://example.com/file_url"
```
```

--------------------------------

### Instantiate a Class-Based Convex Client

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/components/authoring

Demonstrates the basic pattern for instantiating a class-based client for a Convex component, passing the component reference and configuration options.

```javascript
import Foo from "@convex-dev/foo";

import { components } from "./_generated/server.js";



const foo = new Foo(components.foo, {

  maxShards: 10,

});
```

--------------------------------

### Connect MainActivity to Convex Client and UI

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/quickstart/android

Initialize ConvexClient with your deployment URL and set the content of MainActivity to display the Tasks Composable.

```kotlin
val client: ConvexClient by lazy { ConvexClient($YOUR_CONVEX_URL) }



class MainActivity : ComponentActivity() {

  override fun onCreate(savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {

      super.onCreate(savedInstanceState)

      enableEdgeToEdge()

      setContent {

          ConvexQuickstartTheme {

              Scaffold(modifier = Modifier.fillMaxSize()) { innerPadding ->

                  Tasks(

                      modifier = Modifier.padding(innerPadding)

                  )

              }

          }

      }

  }

}
```

--------------------------------

### Convex Object Type Example and Validation

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/database/types

Provides an example of a plain JavaScript object and its validation method for Convex. Objects must not have custom prototypes and have entry limits.

```javascript
{a: "abc"}
```

```javascript
v.object({property: value})
```

--------------------------------

### GET ctx.storage.getMetadata (Deprecated)

Source: https://docs.convex.dev/api/interfaces/server.StorageActionWriter

Deprecated: Get metadata for a file. Use `ctx.db.system.get("_storage", storageId)` instead, which returns equivalent metadata from the `_storage` system table.

```APIDOC
## GET ctx.storage.getMetadata (Deprecated)

### Description
Deprecated: Get metadata for a file. Use `ctx.db.system.get("_storage", storageId)` instead, which returns equivalent metadata from the `_storage` system table (with a slightly different shape).

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
ctx.storage.getMetadata

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **storageId** (GenericId<"_storage">) - Required - The `Id<"_storage">` of the file.

### Request Example
```javascript
const metadata = await ctx.storage.getMetadata(storageId);
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- A `FileMetadata` object if found or `null` if not found.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "_id": "string",
  "_creationTime": 1678886400000,
  "sha256": "string",
  "size": 1024,
  "contentType": "image/jpeg"
}
```
```
